project.title: sigmacadit
project.one_liner: PHP-based medical operations web application with electronic clinical record workflows.
project.description: Monolithic server-rendered PHP application for clinical and administrative operations. Evidence in the repository shows authentication, permission-aware navigation, scheduling, patients, consultations, hospitalization, imaging, laboratory, rehabilitation, pharmacy, users, cash operations, branches, agreements, partner workflows, SQL artifacts, and module-specific Dockerized upload handlers.

prerequisites: PHP with a web server capable of serving this repository as a web root
Docker (optional, only for module-specific upload, image, and document handlers)
setup_steps: No repository-level one-command setup was detected.
Provision a MySQL database using the SQL artifacts in `nuevo-sigma (1).sql`, `todo.sql`, and `agenda/SQL/` as needed.
Configure database connection settings in the PHP files under `Connections/`.
Serve the repository from a PHP-capable web root and open `login.php`.
Use module-local Docker assets only when those upload or image handlers are required.
how_to_run.dev_command: No repository-level start command was detected.
how_to_run.build_command: No repository-level build command was detected.

cv_bullets.bullet: Maintained a PHP-based medical operations platform covering reception, consultations, hospitalization, imaging, laboratory, rehabilitation, pharmacy, administration, cash, and partner workflows.
cv_bullets.notes: Navigation targets and top-level entry pages explicitly enumerate the functional areas.

cv_bullets.bullet: Implemented and maintained role-based navigation and access control, exposing modules according to user permissions, access tiers, and session state.
cv_bullets.notes: The code gates modules by permission strings, validates login state, and prepares per-user session data.

cv_bullets.bullet: Supported appointment scheduling workflows with server-side checks for doctor availability and overlapping bookings.
cv_bullets.notes: A project-specific documentation file describes the scheduling rules and supporting handlers are committed alongside it.

cv_bullets.bullet: Built operational screens and reports around asynchronous PHP endpoints and DataTables-style server-side handlers across multiple modules.
cv_bullets.notes: The app includes first-party `datatable-serverside` directories and DataTables assets referenced from application entry pages.

cv_bullets.bullet: Maintained administrative and reporting surfaces for users, branches, agreements, cash operations, productivity tracking, and insurance-related workflows.
cv_bullets.notes: Menu routing and dedicated entry pages show broad administrative scope beyond clinical modules.

cv_bullets.bullet: Containerized ancillary upload and media handlers with Apache/PHP Docker images for documents, photos, signatures, logos, and clinical images.
cv_bullets.notes: These Docker assets appear in feature-specific folders, indicating containerized support services rather than a repo-wide containerization strategy.

cv_bullets.bullet: Worked in a MySQL-backed PHP codebase that ships SQL schema/data artifacts and mixes PDO with legacy mysql_* access patterns.
cv_bullets.notes: Database access and schema artifacts are explicit in the committed source.

cv_bullets.bullet: Maintained authentication hardening features such as CSRF token validation, remember-me cookies, session fingerprinting, and idle-session expiry.
cv_bullets.notes: Shared auth helpers implement token validation, cookie-based login, session refresh, fingerprint checks, and timeout handling.

red_flags.issue: No repository-level CI, test, lint, formatting, or type-check configuration was detected outside vendored dependencies.
red_flags.notes: Searches for workflow files and common automation configs returned no project-level matches.

red_flags.issue: Database configuration appears to be file-based under `Connections/`; no `.env.example` or similar environment template was detected.
red_flags.notes: The ignore file excludes `Connections/*`, while the application code depends on PHP connection files rather than an environment template.

red_flags.issue: The codebase mixes PDO with deprecated mysql_* APIs.
red_flags.notes: This signals legacy PHP constraints and increases long-term maintenance risk.

red_flags.issue: A large amount of third-party frontend code is committed directly into the repository tree.
red_flags.notes: Vendored assets make project-level manifests and quality signals harder to isolate.
